assets.name: lecture_09_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the best balance of completeness and readability. It preserves the left-hand superspace expression, the middle component expansion, and the lower implication arrow that Susskind is explicitly discussing, while his hand blocks less of the important right-hand expressions than in the earlier candidates. The later frame is similar, but this one leaves the arrow-and-result layout slightly clearer for note writing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: And this arrow doesn't mean, well, it doesn't mean equals.
assets.caption_hint: cubic superfield term and implication arrow

assets.name: lecture_09_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the best overall note-writing value because it preserves both parts of the blackboard argument at once: the boxed equation on the left and the more developed potential-and-derivative relations on the right. It is more complete than the first frame, while avoiding the arm occlusion that becomes more distracting in the later frames. That makes it the clearest documentary snapshot of the layout at this moment.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: It does not require the equations to be not supersymmetric.
